issue_title: Debugging features in the graph dashboard are broken.
issue_body:
Internal debugging features are broken because the `_debuggerDataEnabled` property is [no longer passed to the graph dashboard](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorboard/blob/530310dd26f8f14df7a49b6d8f286c3d63627e0d/tensorboard/components/tf_tensorboard/tf-tensorboard.html#L366). With that property set to false, no debugging features appear.

A long term reality is that the debugger plugin has its own dashboard. For now, we can fix the breakage by having the graph dashboard rely on the `plugins_listing` route to determine whether there is debugging data to visualize. This means that debugging features appear in the graph if the backend (the debugger plugin) detects debugger-related events.

issue_title: Feature request: higher step precision for images etc.
issue_body:
Images in Tensorboard are incredibly useful for us when training generative models as they allow an intuitive feel for what different scalar metrics mean. Currently the number of selectable steps are limited to 10 making it difficult to see transients and instabilities (common in e.g. GANs).

It would be great if we could get a method for **selecting the step for an image with higher precision**. E.g. a manual input for the step would be very helpful, especially if coupled with the ability to scroll around in its immediate area.

Ideally the suggested global step slider in #469 should support this higher precision step selection as well.

issue_title: Certification: Missing DTLS-Alert(close_notify) from commissioner to joiner
issue_body:
After commissioning process is done, Joiner sends a DTLS-Alert(close_notify) message to commissioner, commissioner should reply a DTLS-Alert(close_notify) message to joiner (Spec 8.4.5.3), but this message is not generated in current implementation.

Test Harness verifies this message when running DUT as commissioner tests.

Scenario:
Four NORDIC dev boards with ot-cli-ftd image. The image is based on the commit: 4e92a73. The topology is shown in the following figure. A is leader, B, C and D are routers.
         A
          |
         B
       /    \
      C     D

Issue description:
Both the Node C and D are sending traffic to A at the same time. A moment later, the node A can't receive any packets from node C and D. After the traffic from C and D is stoped,  all the pings from node C , D and A to B fail.  Several minutes later, node B, C and D become leader.

After the traffic is stopped, I use the command "bufferinfo" to see the buffer information on node B:
> bufferinfo
total: 128
free: 0
6lo send: 64 128
6lo reas: 0 0
ip6: 0 0
mpl: 0 0
mle: 0 0
arp: 0 0
coap: 0 0
coap secure: 0 0
application coap: 0 0
Done

The buffer of the node B is full all the time. After I debug the code, I find that the variable mSendBusy in class MeshForwarder is set to TRUE and its value never changed. Which causes messages are blocked in the queue.
Logs for reference:
[4e92a73_A_rx_log.txt](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/files/1851879/4e92a73_A_rx_log.txt)
[4e92a73_B_log.txt](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/files/1851881/4e92a73_B_log.txt)
[4e92a73_C_tx_log.txt](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/files/1851883/4e92a73_C_tx_log.txt)
[4e92a73_D_tx_log.txt](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/files/1851884/4e92a73_D_tx_log.txt)

However, when I try the commit: 302c999, no traffic hang issue happens.
Logs for reference:
[302c999_A_rx_log.txt](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/files/1851923/302c999_A_rx_log.txt)
[302c999_C_tx_log.txt](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/files/1851925/302c999_C_tx_log.txt)
[302c999_D_tx_log.txt](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/files/1851926/302c999_D_tx_log.txt)

In this scenario, I'm using perf to generate traffic. Perf is a tool I wrote to test the performance of Openthread. It only call Timer, otMessage and otUdp API.

If there is noone already working on this tpoic, I'm willing to implement CoAP client with support for retransmission etc.

One question though - shall we provide an API for application to create/send CoAP messages, similar way it is done with a raw UDP?

### Problem

I am trying to read status from a Usb connected esc/pos printer, and it always returns array('B',[0]) or behaves awkwardly (blocks until physical usb disconnect) with timeout expections of libusb1 if I try to send a third argument on device.write or device.read. I don't understand the "interface" parameter to the Usb construction, and its usage as a third argument on device.write, it makes no sense according to pyUsb docs, where it states it should be a timeout. 
### Steps to reproduce
- Remove paper from printer.
- Define the printer e = Usb(0x0XXX,0x0YYY)
- msg=b'\x10\x04\x04'. _DLE EOT 4- Paper Sensor, 8bits, 5 and 6th should  be 1 when paper end_
- e.device.write(e.ep_out, msg)
- e.device.read(e.ep_out,1)
### Device info

 **Printer:** EPSON TM20-II

 **python-escpos version:** 2.0.0

Have there been a discussion about the ipaddress module?

https://docs.python.org/3/library/ipaddress.html

So that instead of strings we can use actually adresses / interfaces / networks?

This would properly ruin backward compability, which I know a lot is fond of - but it could work with an option or something like that, (as in to enable it)

issue_title: Allow to exclude individual objects from sphere analysis

issue_body:
### Steps to Reproduce
1. `virtualenv venv`
2. `source venv/bin/activate`
3. `pip install -e git+https://github.com/googleapis/artman#egg=remote`
4. `start_conductor.py`

### Expected Result
The conductor successfully starts.

### Actual Result
The following dependency conflict occurs:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/google/home/brianwatson/src/gapi-dev/googleapis/venv/bin/start_conductor.py", line 4, in <module>
    __import__('pkg_resources').require('googleapis-artman==0.1.0')
  File "/usr/local/buildtools/current/sitecustomize/sitecustomize.py", line 181, in SetupPathsAndImport
    return real_import(name, globals, locals, fromlist, level)
  File "/usr/local/google/home/brianwatson/src/gapi-dev/googleapis/venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2985, in <module>
    @_call_aside
  File "/usr/local/google/home/brianwatson/src/gapi-dev/googleapis/venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2971, in _call_aside
    f(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/google/home/brianwatson/src/gapi-dev/googleapis/venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2998, in _initialize_master_working_set
    working_set = WorkingSet._build_master()
  File "/usr/local/google/home/brianwatson/src/gapi-dev/googleapis/venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 662, in _build_master
    return cls._build_from_requirements(__requires__)
  File "/usr/local/google/home/brianwatson/src/gapi-dev/googleapis/venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 675, in _build_from_requirements
    dists = ws.resolve(reqs, Environment())
  File "/usr/local/google/home/brianwatson/src/gapi-dev/googleapis/venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 859, in resolve
    raise VersionConflict(dist, req).with_context(dependent_req)
pkg_resources.ContextualVersionConflict: (grpc-google-logging-v2 0.9.3 (/usr/local/google/home/brianwatson/src/gapi-dev/googleapis/venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages), Requirement.parse('grpc-google-logging-v2<0.9.0,>=0.8.1'), set(['gax-google-logging-v2']))
```

### Analysis

This is ultimately because the last release of [`gcloud`](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/gcloud) (`0.18.1`) does not set upper bounds on their `gax-google-*` (now renamed to `gapic-google-*`) and `grpc-google-*` dependencies. Due to the rename of `gax-google-*` to `gapic-google-*`, its latest version is only `0.8.x`. This is incompatible with the latest `0.9.x` versions of `grpc-google-*`.

FYI @tseaver and @dhermes, if you could push a `0.18.2` version of `gcloud` that sets an upper bound of `<0.9.0` on the `gax-google-*` and `grpc-google-*` dependencies, then this issue will be resolved.

issue_title: Reduce or eliminate redundant definitions of dependencies
issue_body:
We currently have dependencies specified in multiple files. Ideally they would only be specified in a single place. I think the right place for these is `setup.py`, but we could/should have a discussion about the single place.

`tox.ini` is the big offender here. Each environment specifies a list of dependencies to install in order to test the package. Instead of:

`deps = 
  mock
  pytest
  pytest-cov
  pytest-mock
`

We should have:

`extras = dev`

which instructs tox to install all the dev dependencies from `setup.py`. `setup.py` is already platform and python version smart about which dependencies are actually needed.

There are some other potential files to examine, but they just install `tox`, so maybe they could be forgiven, but if we can figure out a way...

- appveyor.yml
- travis.yml

issue_title: Remove features from cmd2 which probably don't belong there
issue_body:
There are a number of features currently present in ``cmd2`` which seem like they probably should not be present in a generic framework for creating interactive command line applications.  Instead these features should probably exist in wrapper code present in specific applications which lie on top of ``cmd2``. 

Features included in this ticket include:

- loading scripts from URLs (as opposed to files)
- Default file extension for loading text scripts
- Default file name for load/save

I suspect these features are unused and removing them will simplify code maintenance in the future.  If I am wrong and downstream users of ``cmd2`` are using these features, please get in touch and we can work something out.

issue_title: Piping input from the shell behaves poorly
issue_body:
```$ echo help | python examples/example.py
(Cmd)
Documented commands (type help <topic>):
========================================
_relative_load  help     mumble  pyscript  save  shell      speak
cmdenvironment  history  orate   quit      say   shortcuts
edit            load     py      run       set   show

stty: stdin isn't a terminal
(Cmd) stty: stdin isn't a terminal
```

Notice how the command is not output by **Cmd2**, but the prompt is. This means that the output can not be used as a transcript. There are several options to improve the behavior:

1. Output the prompt and the command. This makes the output a testable transcript.
2. Output neither the prompt or the command. This makes the output easily parseable by other command line utilities that may want to consume only the output. This is the current behavior of the **load** command, which does a similar thing.
3. Make some setting which allows you to choose between option 1 or option 2. Subclasses of **Cmd2** could get this setting from a command line switch, or permanently set it one way or the other.

The other problem are the mysterious 'stty: stdin isn't a terminal' messages. A cursory glance at the code in **_cmdloop** seems to indicate that **Cmd2** always expects to read input from a tty. When we run the **load** command, the commands to run get added to **.cmdqueue** and nothing is read from stdin.

Is there a reason for this?

I was expecting to have shared attributes modified on child to be copied back to parent on child loop exit in order to preserve the change.

What do you think? Maybe this behaviour can be conditioned to some arguments of init method of `AddSubMenu`?

issue_title: Certbot crashes in cleanup for manual CSR for multiple domains with same server root
issue_body:
Apparently, when certbot tries to validate multiple domains in a single pass (e.g. using a custom CSR), if multiple domains share the same root directory, certbot dies with an exception because it has already removed the temporary authorization files from the directory and then tries to remove them a second time and cannot do so.

I've solved this in my copy by changing auth_handler.py:280 from:

            self.auth.cleanup(achalls)

to:

            try :
                self.auth.cleanup(achalls)
            except :
                pass

however, I suspect there's a better way to fix this that would be obvious to somebody who is more familiar with the code.

The name of 3rd party plugins is generated in `certbot/plugins/disco.py`. Currently, all third party plugin names are `<package>:<entry point>`. Because we use the plugin name to prefix the CLI flag name, this generates some really awkward flags `--certbot-foo:bar-baz`. We should do better, but make sure this doesn't break existing plugins by allowing the old form.

issue_title: Mention line in (Apache) conf file in case of Augeas parse/syntax error
issue_body:
See also the community thread [Certbot renew fails on current fedora 24 w/ apache](https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/certbot-renew-fails-on-current-fedora-24-w-apache/25046).

In summary:

When a syntax/parse error occurs when Augeas tries to parse Apache configuration files, `certbot` raises an error and mentions the file in question and the Augeaus error message.

Unfortunately, the *message* itself doesn't contain a line number, so the user has to spit through all the lines, perhaps not even knowing why Augeas complains in the first place. It would be very helpful if the error message also would contain a line number.

On [some blog post](https://stomp.colorado.edu/blog/blog/2011/01/07/on-finding-and-fixing-augeas-parse-errors/) I found Augeas does provide a line number, but this is separate from the message. And `certbot` [only fetches the message, not the line number](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/blob/master/certbot-apache/certbot_apache/augeas_configurator.py#L73).

Could we, for example, change the code to something like:

                msg = (
                    "There has been an error in parsing the file %s on line %s: %s",
                    # Strip off /augeas/files and /error
                    path[13:len(path) - 6], self.aug.get(path + "/line"), self.aug.get(path + "/message"))

If deemed helpful, would it need a check if there's a line number present in the Augeas error? Or does it always come with a line number? (I'm not familiar with Augeas...)

(Untested code by the way :stuck_out_tongue:)
We should have a Courier IMAP IInstaller plugin!

issue_title: Add a timeout to HTTPS connections
issue_body:
In https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/client-takes-almost-one-hour-to-create-certificate/8579/3 and https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/issues/1648#issuecomment-170773846 we've seen users for whom the client is very slow. The root cause winds up being that outbound IPv6 connectivity from their web host is broken in some way.

We should add timeouts to the HTTPS connections, and produce an error message if it takes more than, say, a minute to connect. We could include a few ideas on how to fix, including checking IPv6 connectivity with `wget -6 https://www.google.com` as described at https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/client-takes-almost-one-hour-to-create-certificate/8579/3.

issue_title: phdc-test-agent: MIU size not present in CONNECT frame
issue_body:
phdc-test-agent / p2p Test  03 (fragmentation):  
The initial handshake specifies a MIUX set to 1024 (PN533:  d7 64 fa 00 00 00 00 00 32 46 66 6d 01 01 11 02 02 03 80 03 02 00 03 04 01 32 07 01 03 6b 00) but the *real* value used is 128 (default)  (As this default value (128) is not specified in CONNECT frame (cf pdu.py (282) ), the target is not aware of this change and send 1024 bytes, instead of 128
A workaround is to force the MIUX information (pdu.py - line: 280)
    def to_string(self):
        data = ""
-        if self.miu > 128:
-            miux = self.miu - 128
+       miux = self.miu - 128

issue_title: Should we finally have that celebratory 1.0.0 release?
issue_body:
We've got a new release script, awesome work done by @beardypig and @gravyboat including a HUGE contribution with MPEG DASH support.

In my opinion, streaming / using Streamlink has been super-stable. No major API changes / CLI changes, nor any negative feedback from the community.

Should we celebrate this milestone and finally push a 1.0.0 release out?

Feature request. Compiling latex with the -synctex=1 flag allows TeXStudio (and others) to link the source code and pdf so one can right click on the pdf and "go to source". This is the main thing I miss when using this extension compared to TeXStudio. I'd switch over completely if this extension had synctex support.

issue_title: Lenient parsing mode for unqualified translocations
Does ssh2-python support Dynamic Port Forwarding?
issue_body: I manage to create an socks5 using dynamic port forwarding with assynssh library. I tried to use ssh2-python instead but don't know where to begin. It be great if anyone can show me the way. Thank you very much!

So let us steal, once again (a 3rd, or 4th time), from Bandit.

https://github.com/openstack/bandit/blob/master/bandit/cli/main.py#L157-L160

issue_title: Support IOS-XR named community sets as a reserved word during anonymization
issue_body:
IOS-XR BGP policy allows you to set a BGP community by either:
    a) directly referencing the target community in the config
         `set community 65001:1111`
    b) referencing a community-set in the config
         ` community-set BAR`
         `     65001:1111`
         ` set community BAR`

A sample configuration snippet for community-sets and use in a BGP policy is shown below:

```
community-set LOCAL-NOPREPEND
  1111:2222
end-set
!
community-set LOCAL-LASTRESORT
  1111:4444
end-set
!
community-set LOCAL-HIGHLOCPREF
  1111:1111
end-set
!
!
route-policy FOO
  if destination in BAR or destination in BAZ then
    set community LOCAL-NOPREPEND
    if destination in FOOBAR then
      set community LOCAL-LASTRESORT
    endif
    if destination in BAZBAR then
      set community LOCAL-HIGHLOCPREF
      set community LOCAL-LASTRESORT
    else
      set community LOCAL-NOPREPEND
      set community LOCAL-LASTRESORT
    endif
  else
    drop
  endif
end-policy
```
Netconan will turn that config into:

```
community-set LOCAL-NOPREPEND
  1111:2222
end-set
!
community-set LOCAL-LASTRESORT
  1111:4444
end-set
!
community-set LOCAL-HIGHLOCPREF
  1111:1111
end-set
!
!
route-policy FOO
  if destination in BAR or destination in BAZ then
    set community netconanRemoved42
    if destination in FOOBAR then
      set community netconanRemoved19
    endif
    if destination in BAZBAR then
      set community netconanRemoved20
      set community netconanRemoved21
    else
      set community netconanRemoved22
      set community netconanRemoved23
    endif
  else
    drop
  endif
end-policy
```

issue_title: Inconsistency between with and without toml configuration file scenario
issue_body:
If a toml file (even a very simple one), sources are expected to be in the src folder. However, without a toml file, clang-build will look directly in the target folder. To me this has several disadvantages:
- more heterogenious project structures (some with src folder, others just having the source files directly in the top directory
- restructuring of the project when adding a toml file later, or breaking the default (both are cumbersome)
- messy code

I also see the advantage that clang-build is more usable for quick tests as no special folder structure is required. However, creating a single 'src' folder is not really *effort* and so I am not sure how much this point is actually relevant.

issue_title: Dependent headers are updated AFTER the check if new header dependencies exist

However, the second time, the dependency file is checked to see if some of the headers changed. But the dependency file might be outdated and some new headers made it into the target. This is not captured. Or am I missing something here?

issue_title: Display bug in embedded Output widget - spotted in the context of ipysheet
issue_body:
When embedding [ipysheets](https://github.com/QuantStack/ipysheet) in a widget Output there maybe a display bug.

In short it happens when you want to display an ipysheet, contained in an ipywidget.Output, in an ipywidgets.Tab. Then if this ipysheet is not in the default tab, it won't be properly displayed (typically only the first column shows and a slider appears on the right) until you click on it.

See the display bug in [this notebook](https://nbviewer.jupyter.org/urls/gist.githubusercontent.com/oscar6echo/2a78d98f4f3ff242ac2dd246e7d580b6/raw/b5d658873d897f8a9b76cacc1b11dd90d2f3c4cd/display_bug_widget_tab_output_ipysheet.ipynb).

I understand from @maartenbreddels  that there is a messaging issue in the encapsulation of widgets that results in the Output widget not being 'refreshed' often enough or something along these lines.

This display bug can be circumvented using Box instead of Ouput, or no wrapper widget at all, as shown in the notebook. So it is not critical in my view. However that limitation should be known, and may lead to more annoying problems in other contexts.

# coding=utf-8

import os
import re


def detect_package_root(mod_filename):
    """
    从某个模块的文件路径向上搜索，一直找到这个模块所在包的根目录，将包名第一段返回
    
    :param mod_filename:
    :return: 根包名 or None
    """
    print "xxxinfo: mod_filename = %s" %(mod_filename)
    egginfo_pattern = re.compile(r'.*\.egg-info$')
    print "xxxinfo: egginfo_pattern = %s" %(egginfo_pattern)
    p = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(mod_filename, '..'))
    print "xxxinfo: p = %s" %(p)
    while True:
        ls = os.listdir(p)
        print "xxxinfo: ls = %s" %(ls)
        matched = False
        for f in ls:
            if f == 'setup.py' or egginfo_pattern.match(f):
                print "xxxinfo: f = %s" %(f)
                matched = True
                break
        if matched:
            break
        p_prev = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(p, '..'))
        print "xxxinfo: p_prev = %s" %(p_prev)
        if p == p_prev:
            return None
        p = p_prev
    return p


def get_project_root(test_case_filename):
    return os.getenv("PROJECT_ROOT") or detect_package_root(test_case_filename) or os.getcwd()


def has_override(method, subCls, baseCls):
    return getattr(subCls, method) != getattr(baseCls, method)

issue_title: map中如何只显示两个省，而不是全国
issue_body: map中如何只显示两个省，而不是全国
![screen shot 2018-04-28 at 22 12 27](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4280312/39400908-451878d2-4b31-11e8-992a-5aab84c252f3.png)

Hi,
I'm having difficulties with parsing strings that contain variable expansions like in Bash. `"Hello, $name!"`.
(So this is either a newbie question, or a feature request.)

When I run this example, each character in the string is processed as a separate terminal. It makes sense why it happens but I don't want it to happen. It also seems to decimate performance on a larger input.

```python
import lark

parser = lark.Lark(r'''
IDENTIFIER: /[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z_0-9]*/
string    : "\"" ( CHAR | ESCAPE | expansion ) * "\""
ESCAPE    : "\\" ( "$" | "\"" | CHAR )
expansion : "$" IDENTIFIER
CHAR      : /[^$"\n]/
''', start='string')

tree = parser.parse('"Hello, $name! That will be \$9.95"')
print(tree.pretty())
```
```
string
  H
  e
  l
  l
  o
  ,

  $name
  !

  T
  h
  a
  t

  w
  i
  l
  l

  b
  e

  \$
  9
  .
  9
  5
```

I've tried changing `/[^$"\n]/` to `/[^$"\n]+/` but that causes an error.

Existing suboptimal options I have:

* Accept the poor performance and concatenate characters
* Call a separate string parser recursively during traversal

Note that this is the simplest possible scenario. What about expressions nested in a string, like Ruby's `"Hello, #{arbitrary + expression}!"` or Python's new `f'Hello, {arbitrary + expression}!'`? *python3.lark* doesn't seem to have these, anyway...

issue_title: Should ensure version of 'requests' is compatible with our usage
issue_body: The older Python requests library doesn't support the API we're using. When loading http libraries at runtime, we should ensure that requests is compatible as we do for JSON. As well, should update setup.py appropriately.
